2002-04-01.title: Bomber Strikes Jews and Arabs At Rare Refuge
2002-04-01.abstract: Suicide bomber from Islamic group Hamas blows self up in restaurant in Haifa, Israel, killing self and 14 others and wounding more than 40; attack is among deadliest of 18-month intifada and shocks both Arabs and Jews; Arab-owned Matza restaurant was one of rare places in Israel where Jews felt they could find refuge in company of Arabs; dead include Israeli Arabs; suicide bomber also strikes station for paramedics in settlement of Efrat, south of Bethlehem; kills self and wounds four medics; photos; maps (M)

2002-04-01.title: New Confidence U.S. Has a Qaeda Leader
2002-04-01.abstract: American officials say man believed to be Abu Zubaydah, chief of operations of Osama bin Laden's Al Qaeda terrorist network, was seriously wounded when captured recently in Pakistan and is in US custody; say US is more certain than ever of his identity; capture would represent major accomplishment in American campaign against terrorism, especially since Zubaydah is believed to have taken control of effort to rebuild Al Qaeda network (M)

2002-04-01.title: Residents Cower in Vibrant Ramallah, Now a Ghost Town Occupied by Israelis
2002-04-01.abstract: Once bustling city of Ramallah--commercial, political and intellectual capital of Palestinian West Bank--has become ghost town, quiet except for invading Israeli tanks and armored personnel carriers; soldiers have all but taken over city on third day of siege of Yasir Arafat's compound; siege of city by Israeli forces described; photo (M)

2002-04-01.title: Separatist Leaders in Quebec Shift Their Focus to Stability
2002-04-01.abstract: Leaders of Canada's separatist Parti Quebecois now say they have no intention of calling referendum to declare province's independence from Canada any time in foreseeable future; will refocus on holding on to its control of province's government; is moderating image by building bridges with English-speaking population and pressing central government for greater share of federal spending; shift reflects sharp decline in party's popularity over last several months; recent polls suggest it will have tough time beating back challenge by Liberal Party in next elections, expected within year; its narrow defeat in divisive separatist referendums in 1980 and 1995 recalled (M)

2002-04-01.title: Pakistan's Bold Alliance: U.S. Ties Holding Firm
2002-04-01.abstract: News analysis of Pakistan government's firm cooperation with US in war against terrorism; few countries have done as much; government's willingness to take on strident nationalists and Islamic extremists and recent decision to allow FBI and CIA to conduct raid with Pakistani police in Islamabad for Al Qaeda suspects discussed; while Bush administration has recognized contributions of Gen Pervez Musharraf, it has been slow to deliver on promises of economic aid and other assistance to impoverished country; without some tangible benefits, there is question of how long Musharraf government can continue to mobilize public opinion behind its antiterrorism policies (M)

2002-04-02.title: Sharon Says Arafat Can Leave Ramallah on a "One-Way Ticket"
2002-04-02.abstract: JERUSALEM, April 2 - Israeli Prime Minister Ariel Sharon said on Tuesday he would let Palestinian President Yasser Arafat leave his besieged headquarters in the West Bank city of Ramallah only on condition it was a "one-way ticket."

2002-04-02.title: France Vows Harsh Action After More Synagogues Burn
2002-04-02.abstract: Rising tide of anti-Semitic incidents in France prompts sharp government reaction, with Prime Min Lionel Jospin vowing harsh action after synagogue in Marseilles is destroyed by arson in addition to three others set ablaze over Easter-Passover weekend; he meets with Jewish community leaders; says 1,100 extra police officers will be deployed to guard country's synagogues and Jewish schools; anti-Semitic attacks have soared in past year, reflecting growing radicalization and anger of France's large Muslim population, numbering about five million, as violence in Middle East increases and as Israel applies growing military pressure on Palestinians; photos (M)

2002-04-02.title: Dispute Arises Over a Push To Change Climate Panel
2002-04-02.abstract: Bush administration, after year of lobbying by energy industry, is seeking ouster of Dr Robert T Watson, chief scientist at World Bank and highly regarded climate expert who has for nearly six years directed international panel of hundreds of experts assessing global warming; he is outspoken advocate of idea that human actions, mainly burning of fossil fuels, are contributing to global warming and must be changed to avert environmental upheavals; his term is expiring and State Department has chosen not to renominate him to head panel (M)

2002-04-02.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zimbabwe: Court Frees Journalist
2002-04-02.abstract: Peta Thornycroft, correspondent for Daily Telegraph of London, is freed in Zimbabwe after being detained on charges of working in country illegally (S)

2002-04-02.title: Qaeda and Taliban May Ply Pakistan's Porous Frontier
2002-04-02.abstract: Afghanistan's desolate and remote border region, formed where eastern Afghan province of Khost meets Pakistan, is under intense scrutiny following persistent reports that Al Qaeda and Taliban forces are regrouping to mount guerrilla attacks from sanctuaries across border in Pakistan; recent arrests of suspected Al Qaeda members in areas under tribal control on Pakistani side of border, carried out by Pakistani authorities, give credence to reports that large numbers of fugitive fighters have fled where Americans are unable as yet to reach them; rugged frontier that stretches from legendary Khyber Pass to Quetta is famous for smuggling routes, but gave sanctuary in 1980's to mujahedeen fighters who traveled back and forth across border to stage attacks on Soviet forces; frustrated American officials hint they may reach across frontier to carry out military strikes; Pakistani leaders, who have sent 12,000 troops to border areas, indicate if any military action is needed they will undertake it themselves; photos; maps (M)

2002-04-03.title: Gazans Await Israeli Attack Despite Lack Of a Buildup
2002-04-03.abstract: Sammy Abu Samhadanah, who commands Gaza Strip militia allied with Yasir Arafat, plots strategy if Israel expands military action from West Bank; Israeli security official says Gaza is not of as much concern because recent suicide attacks have been carried out by Palestinians from West Bank (M)

2002-04-03.title: U.S. to Back Scientist From India To Replace Global Warming Expert
2002-04-03.abstract: US State Department meets with industry lobbyists unhappy about international science panel assessing climate change, and then announces support for Indian engineer Dr Rajendra K Pachauri to become panel's new chairman; current chairman Dr Robert T Watson has long pressed for aggressive action to curb heat-trapping gases (S)

2002-04-03.title: NEWS Analysis : Candidates skirt facts on crime in France
2002-04-03.abstract: PARIS:—  The rise of crime in France, a vast concern that may well decide the outcome of the presidential election next month, is one of the areas of French life where everybody senses the extent of the problem, but politicians shut up about the deepest nature of the causes.

2002-04-03.title: World Briefing | Europe: Macedonia: Former Enemies Cooperate
2002-04-03.abstract: Macedonian and ethnic Albanian police patrols resume in two villages that have been strongholds of Albanian guerrillas (S)

2002-04-03.title: Rumsfeld Says Captured Qaeda Leader Won't Be Tortured
2002-04-03.abstract: Defense Secretary Donald H. Rumsfeld rejected suggestions today that Abu Zubaydah might be tortured to get information.

2002-04-04.title: Bush Says U.S. Is to Assume Stronger Role in Ending Violence
2002-04-04.abstract: President Bush said today he was sending Secretary of State Colin L. Powell to the Middle East next week.

2002-04-04.title: World Briefing | Africa: Congo: Summit Meeting On Civil War
2002-04-04.abstract: Leaders of African countries entangled in Congo civil war meet in Zambia (S)

2002-04-04.title: Swamped by Protests, Egypt Cuts Nearly All Ties to Israel
2002-04-04.abstract: Egypt downgrades its relations with Israel in pointed warning that intensifying war against Palestinian Authority of Yasir Arafat is ratcheting up Arab resentment to dangerous levels; Egypt is Israel's largest and most important peace partner; it says it is suspending 'all contacts' with Israeli Government but would keep open diplomatic channel to deal with crisis in West Bank; decision falls short of formally breaking ties, and seems intended as much to placate rising frustration among Egyptian public as to signal anger over widening Israeli military campaign; sixth day of demonstrations at Cairo University pits students demanding expulsion of Israeli Amb Gideon Ben-Ami against riot police; Palestinian leaders are pressing King Abdullah II of Jordan to downgrade his kingdom's ties to Israel as well; photo (M)

2002-04-04.title: AFGHANS ROUND UP HUNDREDS IN PLOT AGAINST LEADERS
2002-04-04.abstract: Afghan security officials say they arrested hundreds of political opponents who were conspiring to mount bombing campaign against government of Hamid Karzai and against former king Mohammad Zahir Shah; Karzai advisers says plot appears to have been aimed at toppling government or destabilizing his regime; say conspiracy is linked to Gulbuddin Hekmatyar, longtime warlord known for his anti-Western views and ruthlessness on battlefield; he was last seen in Pakistan and Iran; with details of alleged plot so sketchy, roundup of well-known opponents of Karzai's government seems certain to prompt suspicions that government fabricated threat to crush its opponents; wave of arrests illustrates fragility of American-backed effort to foster democracy in country with long tradition of cutthroat and tyrannical politics; photo (M)

2002-04-04.title: Tokyo Journal; Sushi Comes Home, With Cream Cheese and Chili
2002-04-04.abstract: American-style sushi is being served with great success at restaurants in Japan, sushi's country of origin; this culinary cross-fertilization illustrates high-speed ricochet of American and other influences across Pacific; not long ago most Americans considered sushi to be far too exotic; photos; map (M)

2002-04-05.title: BUSH DEMANDS ISRAELI PULLOUT; INSISTS ARAFAT OPPOSE TERROR; POWELL WILL GO TO THE MIDEAST
2002-04-05.abstract: Pres Bush thrusts his administration into central role in Middle East crisis, demanding that Israel withdraw from West Bank and telling Arab nations and Yasir Arafat that they must actively oppose terrorism if they hope to create Palestinian state, speech in Rose Garden; says he will send Secretary of State Colin L Powell to region next week; Bush aides say speech sets president on course of acting as central mediator in peace effort, role he previously sought to avoid; say he decided to intervene more directly because he fears that long-term allies in region like Egypt and Jordan are facing destabilizing protests over Israeli-Palestinian conflict; senior aide to Yasir Arafat says Arafat accepts Bush's remarks 'without conditions'; in Israel, Prime Min Ariel Sharon says military operation in West Bank will continue and that he will refuse to negotiate before terror is subdued; Bush's speech seeks to strike far more delicate balance than he has in comments of recent weeks, when he seemed to be giving Israel virtual green light to defend itself however it deems necessary; Bush now says Israeli settlement activity in occupied territories must stop and occupation must end; he calls on Israel to show respect for dignity of Palestinian people; Bush spares no one--not Israel, Palestinians or Arab nations--in his criticism or his challenges; photos (L)

2002-04-05.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-04-05.abstract: Asia North KOREA: Public Enemy NO. 1 ; MYANMAR: Freedom, Some Day, For Dissident; INDIA: Accusations Of Brutality ; NEPAL: State Of Emergency Eased.

2002-04-05.title: Angry and Ashamed, Indian Prime Minister Tours Riot-Torn State
2002-04-05.abstract: Indian Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee visits riot-torn state of Gujarat and pleads for end to violence that has pitted Muslims against Hindus and left at least 815 people dead; speaks angrily about fact that some people have been burned to death; says he is shamed by taint of such violence; many Muslims are disappointed that he did not chastise Narendra Modi, Hindu nationalist chief minister in state who has been accused of turning blind eye to horrendous rampages against Muslims during first days of violence; photo (M)

2002-04-05.title: Indonesia General Denies Responsibility for East Timor Killings
2002-04-05.abstract: Gen Wiranto, commander of Indonesian Army when militias backed by military rampaged through East Timor three years ago, killing more than 100 people, says he was not responsible for 'the incident'; blames United Nations and East Timorese police for violence that followed referendum in which majority of East Timorese voted for independence; independent commission in Indonesia and UN panel concluded two years ago that military, which opposed independence, supported militias; Wiranto appears as witness, not defendant, at trial on human rights abuses by military in East Timor; five mid-level military officers as well as police officers and civilian leaders are defendants; trial has direct bearing on Indonesia's relations with US, where Congressional restrictions are placed on American aid to Indonesian military because of human rights abuses in East Timor (M)

2002-04-05.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-04-05.abstract: Europe SPAIN: Europe-asia Meeting On Migrants; FRANCE: Ruling On Diana's Death Upheld.

2002-04-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: I.R.A. Accused In Break-In
2002-04-06.abstract: Police in Northern Ireland make three arrests and charge Irish Republican Army was behind break-in at Special Branch headquarters in which intelligence data about undercover operations and informants was stolen; Sinn Fein, IRA's political wing, scores arrests as 'smoke screen' to hide security service role in theft (S)

2002-04-06.title: Bomb Is Defused at Entrance to Jewish Cemetery in France
2002-04-06.abstract: Bomb is found and defused at entrance to Jewish cemetery, Strasbourg, France; police arrest five people in connection with firebomb thrown at suburban Paris synagogue; say three people suspected of hurling firebombs at Montpellier synagogue have confessed; police action seems to reflect urgency felt by Prime Min Lionel Jospin's government in face of complaints that there has not been enough public outcry over anti-Semitic attacks; judicial officials probe arson charges against at least dozen men who crashed two cars through gates of Lyon synagogue and set them on fire; 21 prominent French Jews publicly denounce Israel's occupation of Palestinian West Bank cities; photo (M)

2002-04-06.title: Germans of One Mind About Two Rivals: Dubious
Pentagon to Cut Navy Role in Afghan War
2002-04-06.abstract: United States officials say Pentagon has ordered cut in naval forces committed to war in Afghanistan in sign that peak fighting there has passed and that military is adjusting its troops for long-term global war on terrorism; say US will withdraw one of two aircraft carriers and half of 4,000 marines aboard ships from northern Arabian Sea; other signs that military prepares for long haul in Afghanistan noted; photo (M)

2002-04-06.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-04-06.abstract: Africa NIGERIA: Court Sides With Government On Oil; SUDAN: Cease-fire Monitors.

2002-04-07.title: Israel Presses On With Attacks, Focusing on Northern West Bank
2002-04-07.abstract: Israeli forces and Palestinian militants intensify their fighting in Nablus and Jenin on West Bank; Israeli officials interpret Pres Bush's latest demand that Israel withdraw from West Bank without delay as providing them sufficient scope to continue operation, at least until Sec of State Colin L Powell arrives in Israel on truce-seeking mission; photos (M)

2002-04-07.title: For Dogged German Challenger, It's Still About the Economy
2002-04-07.abstract: Edmund Stoiber makes malaise of German economy his core issue in campaign to unseat Chancellor Gerhard Schroder in Sept 22 elections; Stoiber, candidate of Christian Democratic Union and his own Christian Social Union, gives interview with American journalists in advance of visit to Washington in which he tries to show his familiarity with foreign policy, urges even closer relationship with US and works to undercut any idea that he is provincial xenophobe, while defending restrictions on immigration; his perception as dour bureaucrat and 'Bavarian pit bull' puts him at disadvantage with the personable, telegenic Schroder; photo (M)

2002-04-07.title: U.S. Envoy Campaigns For Trade Pacts On Asian Trip
2002-04-07.abstract: US trade representative, Robert B Zoellick, tours high-tech plants in Indonesia to show that countries of Southeast Asia can stand up to looming economic force of China through trade cooperation; US hopes to right what it sees as unhealthy imbalance between China and smaller countries to south by urging them to reduce trade barriers among themselves to attract more outside investment (M)

2002-04-07.title: Allies Discuss Terrorism and the Middle East: Bush and Blair on Policy
2002-04-07.abstract: Excerpts from news conference held by Pres Bush and Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain in Crawford, Tex; photos (M)

2002-04-07.title: Vowing to Stay at a Holy Site As Troops and Impasse Linger
2002-04-07.abstract: Palestinian governor of Bethlehem, Muhammad Madani, vows that neither he nor any of other Palestinians holed up inside Church of Nativity will leave until Israeli troops end their five-day siege; an archbishop, two nuns and two female Palestinian church employees leave church, which holds about 150 Palestinians, 40 priests and other church employees; Israelis say they have no intention of firing on church or harming anyone inside -- as long as any wanted gunmen inside are turned over to them; photo (M)

2002-04-08.title: Shattered Afghan Families Demand U.S. Compensation
2002-04-08.abstract: Victims of bombing in Afghanistan hand in petitions from 400 families at American Embassy in Kabul, part of growing movement to demand compensation from US for loss of families and homes; represent some of possibly thousands of civilians who suffered in campaign against Taliban and Al Qaeda; Global Exchange, human rights organization supporting victims and their claims, estimates about 2,000 families may have suffered losses in bombings; photo (M)

2002-04-08.title: Still a Standoff in Diplomacy, Too
2002-04-08.abstract: Still a Standoff in Diplomacy, Too

2002-04-08.title: Popular Arab Anger: A Long-Term Threat to the Foundations of Peace
2002-04-08.abstract: News analysis; Arab anger and demonstrations across Middle East do not represent immediate threat to moderate governments that have served as America's principal friends and allies in region, but unrelenting siege by Israeli forces in occupied West Bank and at Ramallah headquarters of Yasir Arafat may be radicalizing politics of region and creating threat to peace and recognition of Jewish state by rising generation of Arabs (M)

2002-04-08.title: Hungary Socialists Win Narrowly in Early Parliamentary Vote
2002-04-08.abstract: Voters in Hungary hand slim but unexpected defeat to governing center-right leaders in first round of parliamentary elections, setting stage for even tougher final round of voting in two weeks; reject outright place in Parliament for far-right Freedom and Justice Party, whose possible voice in future government was major question of campaign; photo (M)

2002-04-08.title: Text of Speech by Sharon to Israeli Parliament
2002-04-08.abstract: Following is the text of a speech by Ariel Sharon to a special session of the Israeli Parliament, as provided by the Prime Minister's Office.

2002-04-09.title: 13 Israeli Soldiers Killed; Sharon Says Offensive Will Go On
2002-04-09.abstract: After 13 Israeli soldiers were killed in Jenin, Prime Minister Ariel Sharon said today that Israel's military offensive in the West Bank would continue.

2002-04-09.title: U.S. to Send More G.I.'s to Yemen and Philippines
2002-04-09.abstract: Pentagon prepares to increase its commitment to global war on terror by readying new troops for deployment to Yemen and Philippines; logistics assessment team already in Yemen is paving way for arrival of as many as 150 American troops; initial group of 50 to 70 soldiers will be sent by end of month, and will consist predominantly of Special Forces troops; will also include seaport and airport security specialists; training by Navy and Coast Guard of Yemeni forces that patrol 1,500-mile-long coast noted; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says there are no plans to resume port calls in Yemeni port of Aden, where terrorists bombed destroyer Cole 16 months ago killing 16 American sailors; presence of about 575 American troops in Philippines advising and training local troops to combat Abu Sayyaf rebels on Basilan island noted; Rumsfeld says he is awaiting final proposal from military advisers for deployment of additional troops, mostly engineers and civilian affairs specialists (S)

2002-04-09.title: I.R.A.'s Illegal Arsenal Reduced In a 2nd Act of Disarmament
2002-04-09.abstract: International commission charged with disarming Irish Republican Army says organization has for second time removed batch of weapons, ammunition and explosives from its illegal arsenal (M)

2002-04-09.title: Bomb Kills 4 In Jalalabad But Minister Is Uninjured
2002-04-09.abstract: Afghanistan's defense minister Muhammad Fahim escapes apparent assassination attempt unhurt as bomb explodes in crowd lining streets of Jalalabad to welcome him for visit; four people are killed and 18 are wounded; attack is seen as latest incident in organized effort to undermine American-backed government of Hamid Karzai, which took office in December (M)

2002-04-09.title: Powell Affirms That He Intends to Meet Arafat
2002-04-09.abstract: Secretary of State Colin L. Powell said he intended to meet with Yasir Arafat this weekend. Mr. Powell plans to meet with Mr. Sharon in Jerusalem on Friday.

2002-04-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Yugoslavia: Restructuring Plan Advances
2002-04-10.abstract: Parliaments of Serbia and Montenegro approve plan to remodel Yugoslavia as new state called Serbia and Montenegro (S)

2002-04-10.title: In Mexico, Greed Kills Fish by the Seaful
2002-04-10.abstract: Greed and corruption are allowing Mexican fishing fleets to drain Gulf of California, decimating every important species and leaving impoverished local fishermen with meager catches; photos; American and Japanese ships were first to exploit waters as many governments pour subsidies into commercial fishing fleets, causing fisheries to collapse around world; Gulf of California is world's youngest sea, created when San Andreas fault split Mexico millions of years ago and let Pacific pour in; waters are home home to 875 species of fish and 30 species of marine mammal, which have been killed indiscriminately for years (M)

2002-04-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Policeman Killed In 'Road Rage'
2002-04-10.abstract: Focus of French presidential election is likely to remain on law and order following death of police officer in Vannes; gunman apparently furious over driving incident sprayed bullets into police station (S)

2002-04-10.title: Musharraf Makes Debut On the Stump
2002-04-10.abstract: Pakistan Pres Pervez Musharraf opens one-man campaign to remain in office for five years, delivering crisp, rousing speech at huge organized rally in Islamabad; Musharraf is exempt from ban on public rallies because he has no political party and is ostensibly not waging political campaign (M)

2002-04-10.title: In New Rebuff to U.S., Sharon Presses Military Sweep
2002-04-10.abstract: Prime Minister Ariel Sharon vowed to continue Israel's military sweep through the West Bank after a Palestinian suicide bomber attacked an Israeli bus today.

2002-04-11.title: Convoy Struck in November Found to Lack U.N. Markings
2002-04-11.abstract: United Nations food convoy that was heavily damaged on Nov 10 during American airstrike in Afghanistan was unmarked and had not coordinated its travel with US-led coalition forces, according to investigation by Central Command; convoy was en route to city of Bamian and struck by two bombs that were dropped in effort to block Taliban and Al Qaeda forces; no United Nations employees were hurt in incident; two reports are also released on Afghan detainees who claimed they had been beaten while in custody, but report says injuries are consistent with force necessary to secure them (M)

2002-04-11.title: Spotlight on Central Asia Is Finding Repression, Too
2002-04-11.abstract: Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan and Tajikistan, impoverished former Soviet republics of Central Asia, are undergoing surge of repression and corruption just as they are attracting new world attention as United States allies in its war on terrorism; authoritarian turn in region is problem for US, which relies on republics' support for military operations in Afghanistan; Bush administration has criticized human rights records of several governments but has been careful not to undermine them; several nations crack down harder than usual on political opponents, even as World Economic Forum's Eurasian Summit attracts hundreds of Western business executives and government officials to Kazakhstan (M)

2002-04-11.title: Afghan Refugees Allowed to Pass After Poppy Farmers End Protest
2002-04-11.abstract: Thousands of Afghan refugees begin trek home across country's eastern border after poppy farmers, at government's behest, call off protest that had blocked refugees' passage; however, fighting at western border and confusing situation in east highlights tenuous hold that interim government headed by Hamid Karzai exerts over fractious and war-torn country; poppy farmers can earn much more by taking crops to market than government is willing to pay them to destroy harvest although there is confusion about amount government would pay; photo (M)

2002-04-11.title: Fresh Attacks, And Sniper Fire
2002-04-11.abstract: WEST BANK -- After a suicide bombing in Haifa that killed eight people, four of them soldiers, Ariel Sharon vowed that Israel would continue with its military sweep. He made the declaration to soldiers near Jenin, a day ahead of Colin L. Powell's arrival, asserting that the United States should not ''put any pressures upon us'' to withdraw.    Defense Minister Benjamin Ben-Eliezer said Israel would not interfere with Secretary Powell's plans to visit Yasir Arafat. ''As long as Powell wants to see Arafat he can see him,'' Mr. Ben-Eliezer said, adding, ''We are not preventing him to see Arafat.''

2002-04-11.title: Egyptians Protest Against Israel and U.S., but Fear Takes Edge Off Bravado
2002-04-11.abstract: Egyptian students demonstrate, Alexandria, against Israel, United States support for Israel, and Egyptian government's violence against and arrest of protesters; crowd, fearful of police, begins to disperse within hours; photo (M)

2002-04-12.title: World Briefing | Asia: Nepal: King Signs Antiterror Law
2002-04-12.abstract: King Gyanendra of Nepal signs antiterrorist law passed by Parliament as security forces fight Maoist rebels trying to overthrow constitutional monarchy; king has eased state of emergency that had imposed restrictions on political parties and press (S)

2002-04-12.title: White House Hints Powell May Cancel Meeting With Arafat
2002-04-12.abstract: The Bush administration signaled that Colin L. Powell might cancel his meeting with Yasir Arafat unless he denounced terrorism.

2002-04-12.title: An Arrival, But No Pullout
2002-04-12.abstract: ISRAEL: Secretary of State Colin L. Powell arrived, planning a ''very long conversation'' with Prime Minister Ariel Sharon over his flouting of American demands for a prompt Israeli withdrawal from West Bank cities and towns.    WEST BANK: Israeli forces withdrew from several Palestinian villages but continued to occupy Ramallah, Bethlehem, Jenin and Nablus, and encircled Tulkarm and Qalqilya. Mr. Sharon gave no signs that he planned to lift the siege, citing men still wanted in the area.

2002-04-12.title: Day by Day, Arab Shells Fray Nerves In the Golan
2002-04-12.abstract: Iranian-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, in solidarity with Palestinians, attack Israeli positions in Golan Heights and Israeli soldiers respond, threatening to open second front; Israeli leaders warn Syria, main power broker in Lebanon, that they hold it responsible for renewed hostilities and that harsh retaliation is possible in Lebanon if attacks continue; residents of Kiryat Shmona, on Israel's northern border, have frayed nerves after two years of calm since withdrawal from Lebanon (M)

2002-04-12.title: Clerics Tell Of Ritual And Danger Under Siege
2002-04-12.abstract: Detailed portrait of life inside Church of Nativity compound after nine days of Israeli siege emerges in telephone interviews from inside complex with three people: Father Parthenios, Greek Orthodox monk, Rev Amjad Sabbara, pastor of Roman Catholic church in complex and Muhammad al-Madani, Palestinian governor of Bethlehem who is hiding in church; all three men deny that 40 priests and monks and 5 nuns in complex are being held hostage by Palestinian fighters holed up with them, as Israel contends; they warn that food is in very short supply and will not last another day or two; say explosions and gunfire regularly surround church; photo; maps (M)

2002-04-13.title: The hippest hangouts in Tokyo
2002-04-13.abstract: TOKYO—  For all intents and purposes, Tokyo is an ever-changing theme park whose operating motto is: Scrap and Rebuild. From office building monstrosities to shops, clubs and restaurants, nothing lasts very long before it is torn down to give way to something new.

2002-04-13.title: Soldier Gets Life Sentence For Drowning Her 2 Children
2002-04-13.abstract: Specialist Lillie Morgan, United States Army finance clerk who drowned her small children last September in what she says was attempt to avenge her husband's suspected unfaithfulness, is sentenced by military court to life in prison, Mannheim, Germany (S)

2002-04-13.title: A Late Entry Quickly Hits Mideast Wall
2002-04-13.abstract: Bush administration finds itself confronted by defiant Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon and by quandary of whether to meet Yasir Arafat just as Arafat's allies claim responsibility for another suicide bombing; in short, eight days into his first real involvement in Mideast conflict, Pres Bush seems bogged down in morass he has tried to avoid for 15 months; photos (News Analysis) (M)

2002-04-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Yugoslavia: Official Found Dead
2002-04-13.abstract: Miodrag Kovac, Yugoslavia's minister for laor, health and social policy, is found hanged by his own belt in bathroom of Madrid hotel hours after addressing United Nations Assembly on Aging; Kovac reportedly was upset over press criticism; recent reports in Montenegro accused him of financial wrongdoing (S)

2002-04-13.title: Gang Attacks Jews on Sports Field in France

2002-04-14.title: In Pakistan's Interior, A Troubling Victory In Hunt for Al Qaeda
2002-04-14.abstract: Faisalabad, industrial city deep in interior of Pakistan, has not been high on list of places considered hideouts for Al Qaeda terrorists, until FBI agents and Pakistani police raided house on outskirts and arrested some twenty Arab and other captives, including Abu Zubaydah, highest-ranking Al Qaeda figure captured since Sept 11 attacks; US has confirmed that Zubaydah was seriously injured in shootout, but said little else; Faisalabad does not have turbulent reputation of Pakistani cities that are factories of Islamic extremism; accounts of men who rented home reviewed; photo; map (M)

2002-04-14.title: Limits of Force: Superior Israeli Firepower Isn't Likely to End Terror
2002-04-14.abstract: Military analysis says Israeli operation in West Bank is sweeping counterinsurgency campaign that, given enough time, could reduce though not end Palestinian terrorist attacks; Israeli troops combing towns and refugee camps have arrested or killed militants terrorist suspects, seized hundreds of weapons, uncovered dozens of bomb-making factories and retrieved explosive belts devised for bombers; all-out conflict with Arabs is considered unlikely: Israel's military, though outnumbered, is qualitatively far superior; photo; map; comparison table (M)

2002-04-14.title: Toll in Tunisia Blast Rises
2002-04-14.abstract: Death toll from truck explosion outside ancient synagogue in Djerba, Tunisia, rises to 13, including eight German tourists; authorities agree to allow German officials to join probe of whether incident was accident, and express support for Tunisia's Jewish population (S)

2002-04-14.title: Arafat Condemns Terror; Powell Meeting On
2002-04-14.abstract: Sec Colin Powell says he will meet with Yasir Arafat, after Palestinian leader issues statement condemning terrorist acts against civilians and 'especially' suicide bombing that killed six people in Jerusalem; Arafat's statement, demanded by White House as condition for Powell meeting, also rails at length against Israel; Powell to see Arafat in Ramallah compound where he has been sealed up with about 100 other people by Israeli troops; suicide bomber, identified as Andaleeb Taqtaqah, blew herself up outside Mahane Yehuda market in Jerusalem; photos (M)

2002-04-14.title: Southeast Asia's Press Is Under Pressure
2002-04-14.abstract: Recent television blackout imposed by Thai government is latest evidence of dwindling hopes for more open press in Southeast Asia, where governments show little patience with press freedom; Malaysia, which has repeatedly hamstrung newspapers, and Bangladesh have banned certain Western magazines with unflattering articles; Indonesia, by contrast, is said to suffer from too much press of inferior quality; about 600 newspapers, many of them tabloid retailers of rumor and innuendo, have opened since fall of Pres Suharto (M)

2002-04-15.title: Nervous Markets Fear Surge in the Price of Oil
2002-04-15.abstract: Crude oil prices expected to surge when trading begins on commodity markets on April 15 after weekend lull; oil markets confront possibility of continuing political instability in Venezuela after whiplash turn of events that brought Pres Hugo Chavez back to power following coup on April 11 (S)

2002-04-15.title: Bin Laden Makes Appearance on New Video
2002-04-15.abstract: Osama bin Laden, his deputy and a man believed to have been one of the Sept. 11 hijackers appear on a videotape that has just surfaced.

2002-04-15.title: Inside a Siege, Signs of Life and Loss
2002-04-15.abstract: Conditions in and around devastated Ramallah headquarters of Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat described; siege has left Arafat and few dozen aides, sympathizers and international peace protestors virtually isolated in few rooms--often without electricity, water or working plumbing; photo (M)

2002-04-15.title: No Cease-Fire Until Withdrawal, Arafat Tells Powell
2002-04-15.abstract: Sec of State Colin L Powell meets with Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat in largely destroyed headquarters in Ramallah, Arafat's first meeting with top American official since Israel declared him irrelevant months ago; Arafat tells Powell no cease-fire is possible until Israel ends military operation in West Bank and withdraws from cities and towns it rcently occupied; Powell reportedly delivers blunt message, warning Arafat of need to make changes in interests of peace; later meets with Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon, who proposes international summit on Middle East along lines of 1991 Madrid conference but without participation of Arafat; presses Pres Bush's request for full Israeli withdrawal; photos; map (M)

2002-04-15.title: U.S. Treatment of War Captives Is Criticized
2002-04-15.abstract: Amnesty International issues harsh evaluation of American policy, accusing US of violating several international laws in treatment of prisoners captured during war in Afghanistan and of failing to live up to its assertions that it is complying with Geneva Convention (M)

2002-04-16.title: In Afghan Villages, Elders Begin an Ancient Process to Choose a National Government
2002-04-16.abstract: Afghans embark on complicated and lengthy process of choosing new leaders, nearly five months after Taliban forces were swept from power; every village in land will be consulted; will put forward one or more representatives to go forward to regional gatherings, which will select 1,500 people to attend loya jirga, or grand council, in Kabul from June 10 through 16; council will formally be convened by former King Mohammed Zahir Shah, set to return to Kabul within days after 29 years in exile (M)

2002-04-16.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-04-16.abstract: Europe RUSSIA: Abuses In Chechnya Cited; YUGOSLAVIA: Progress In Extradition Talks ; BRUSSELS: Nato And Russia Discuss Pact Nato'.

2002-04-16.title: World Briefing: Americas
2002-04-16.abstract: Americas PARAGUAY: Ex-president Convicted Of Corruption; BRAZIL: Candidate Switches Races.

2002-04-16.title: ISRAELIS CAPTURE AN ARAFAT DEPUTY IN THE WEST BANK
2002-04-16.abstract: Israeli forces arrest Marwan Barghouti, leader of Yasir Arafat's Fatah movement in biggest catch of its West Bank military offensive; he is accused of planning and financing terrorist attacks while masquerading as politician without military role; Prime Min Ariel Sharon says capture is evidence of value of Israeli mission; says Israel will try Barghouti, who is brought to Jerusalem for questioning; Palestinians insist Barghouti is not military man; warn arrest is likely to provoke more violence; arrest deprives Arafat of trusted lieutenant while further complicating Sec of State Colin Powell's effort to arrange truce after more than 18 months of fighting; arc of Barghouti's career--from Israeli prisoner, to peace advocate, to supporter of conflict to Israeli prisoner again--discussed; once popular with Israeli officials, he is now simply regarded as terrorist; Sharon announces Israeli forces will withdraw within week from Nablus and Jenin, two West Bank cities where fighting has been fiercest, but again defies American demands that Israeli military operation end; says military will remain in Bethlehem and Ramallah until it suceeds in capturing or exiling wanted men trapped in each city; speaks with Pres Bush by telephone; tells him plans for withdrawal from Nablus and Jenin; photos (M)

2002-04-16.title: Israel Pursues Military Actions as Powell Prepares to Leave
2002-04-16.abstract: As Secretary of State Colin L. Powell prepared to end his peace mission, Ariel Sharon made clear that the Israeli dragnet through the West Bank was not over.

2002-04-17.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: One-Day National Strike
2002-04-17.abstract: Banks and state-owned companies in India are closed by one-day strike by estimated 10 million workers protesting sale of government companies and changing laws to make layoffs easier (S)

2002-04-17.title: Powell Style: Keep Temperature Down
2002-04-17.abstract: Sec Colin Powell, canny veteran of Washington's bureaucratic battles, concludes Mideast peace venture with comments making clear point that he may be Bush administration's front-line negotiator, but he is far from free-lancer, as some administration snipers back home would imply; photo; Powell's personal approach is unwaveringly calm, upbeat, low-key, good-humored, and unrevealing; Israeli and Palestinian negotiators agree he listens and talks candidly and patiently (M)

2002-04-17.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Neighbors Of U.S. Base Lose Suit
2002-04-17.abstract: People living near US air base in western suburb of Tokyo lose final appeal in noise pollution case against US government (S)

2002-04-17.title: Shredding of Smoking Data Is Ruled Deliberate
2002-04-17.abstract: Australian court rules that British American Tobacco did in fact carry out systematic worldwide program of destroying papers that documented health risks of smoking; ruling, first after years of unproven allegations, finds that shredding began in 1985, supervised by then-counsel, at time when American subsidiary faced increasing number of lawsuits; implications for US cases discusssed (M)

2002-04-17.title: Escapee, 16, Tells of Stench, Cold and Sleepless Nights in Besieged Church
2002-04-17.abstract: Jihad Abdul Rahman, 16, escapes from Church of Nativity in Bethlehem, where some 250 Palestinian gunmen are holed up surrounded by Israeli forces, and tells of meager rations, cold, smell of rotting bodies and gangrenous wounds, and Israeli loudspeakers blaring through sleepless nights; Bethlehem governor Muhammad Madani, inside church, says Yasir Arafat has instructed him to form committee to meet with Israelis; he and monks reached by telephone deny they are held hostage by gunmen; Israelis evacuate two of wounded (M)

2002-04-18.title: POWELL ENDS TRIP; SEES NO CEASE-FIRE BEFORE A PULLOUT
2002-04-18.abstract: Secretary of State Colin Powell leaves Israel without cease-fire he came for; says no truce could take hold until Israel withdraws from Palestinian-controlled cities and towns its forces have seized; antagonists seem angrier and more fearful of each other, and with less ground for hope that even high-level American intervention could force halt to violence by either side; Powell ends his six-day trip with harsh words for Israel and for Palestinians and their leader Yasir Arafat; some Israeli officials express satisfaction with his trip, saying he helped calm fighting along Israel's northern border; Palestinian officials bitterly assess his mission as failure, blaming Prime Min Ariel Sharon; Arab nations express frustration that US has not put more pressure on Sharon; Pres Hosni Mubarak of Egypt, one of few Arab nations that have been allied with US, cancels meeting with Powell during stopover in Cairo, sending his foreign minister instead; photo (M)

2002-04-18.title: A Rising Toll for Bush: No Peace, More Blame
2002-04-18.abstract: Analysis: apparent failure of Secretary of State Colin Powell's mission to Mideast has now pushed American diplomacy into region's most intractable conflict to point where political costs at home are rising and diplomatic costs with Arab world are steep, and where even global antiterror campaign could be in jeopardy; photo (M)

2002-04-18.title: Iran Executes Worker Who Strangled 16 Women Over 2 Years
2002-04-18.abstract: Iranian state radio reports execution of Saeed Hanai, who was convicted of strangling 16 women in city of Mashad over two years (M)

2002-04-18.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: Vietnamese Refugees Leave Camps
2002-04-18.abstract: United Nations moves 905 ethnic minority Vietnamese refugees from two camps just inside Cambodia to Phnom Penh in preparation for their resettlement in United States; Cambodia plans to close camps and seal borders to new asylum seekers, hill tribe members formerly known as Montagnards, who have been fleeing violent land disputes and government crackdown on their unauthorized Protestant Christian groups (S)

2002-04-18.title: Bush Sets Role for U.S. in Afghan Rebuilding
2002-04-18.abstract: President Bush on Wednesday embraced a major American role in rebuilding Afghanistan, calling for an effort he compared to the Marshall Plan.

2002-04-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Greece: Nazi-Era Case Postponed
2002-04-19.abstract: Special Supreme Court tribunal in Athens defers ruling on whether it has jurisdiction to try class-action suit by about 60,000 Greeks demanding compensation from Germany for Nazi atrocities in more than 60 Greek villages between 1941 and 1944 (S)

2002-04-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Greece: 3 Held For Smuggling Migrants
2002-04-19.abstract: Grek authorities report arrest of three Turks for smuggling boatload of illegal immigrants seeking to reach Greece, then abandoning wooden fishing boat when it capsized in stormy seas off Naxos; two passengers drowned and more are feared dead (S)

2002-04-19.title: U.S. Error Kills 4 Canadians in Afghanistan
2002-04-19.abstract: American fighter pilot mistakenly drops 500-pound bomb on group of Canadian soldiers conducting training mission in southern Afghanistan, killing four and wounding eight; Pentagon says accident occurred after pilots of two F-16's saw muzzle flashes on ground below and thought they were under attack; it is not clear whether pilots were given permission to attack or independently dropped bomb in what they believed was self-defense; Canadian military officials say training area is well known to American forces; Pres Bush calls Prime Min Jean Chretien of Canada to express condolences and to pledge full investigation of incident with Canadians; central issue for investigators will be whether pilots of F-16 team sought permission from air controllers to attack (M)

2002-04-19.title: French Never-Say-Die Rightist Grasps at Top Spot a 5th Time
2002-04-19.abstract: Jean-Marie Le Pen, 73, is running for president of France for fifth time; victory is not considered within his grasp, but he is crowing with triumph that steadily rising crime and recent wave of anti-Semitic attacks by French Arab youths have mainstream politicians echoing his law-and-order positions and more voters agreeing with his anti-immigrant views; he has tempered some positions, but he still wants 'native French' given priority for jobs and welfare, and he has called for revival of capital punishment and for France to quit European Union; in this election, with so little enthusiasm for front-runners Prime Min Lionel Jospin and Pres Jacques Chirac, anything better than 10 percent could put Le Pen in powerful position in May 5 runoff; photo (M)

2002-04-19.title: Now on the Spot, Builders Of Beit El Vow to Continue
2002-04-19.abstract: Israelis in Beit El in West Bank are defiant about expanding their settlement, even after Secretary of State Colin L Powell urges reconsideration of settlers' presence in Gaza Strip and West Bank; since Jewish settlers moved onto hill in 1970's, Beit El and nearby Arab neighborhoods have become places of striking contrasts; settlement resembles middle-class American suburb, while Arab zone is comprised of grim neighborhods connected by dirt roads; settlers note that land was barren before they built settlement; policy of Israel's current Government is to refrain from building new settlements while allowing current settlements to grow naturally; Beit El's settlers plan construction as they see fit; photos; map (M)

2002-04-20.title: U.S. Sends More Troops To Philippines In Rebel Fight
2002-04-20.abstract: 340 US Navy engineers and Marine Corps guardsare in Philippines to build roads and landing pads and dig wells for Filipino and US troops fighting Abu Sayyaf guerrillas on Basilan island (S)

2002-04-20.title: Article 2002042093545921473 -- No Title
2002-04-20.abstract: function pop_me_up(pURL, features){new_window = window.open(pURL, "popup_window", features);new_window.focus();}  function changeImage(image_name,image_src) {document.images[image_name].src = image_src;}  function MM_jumpMenu(targ,selObj,restore){ //v3.0eval(targ+".location='"+selObj.options[selObj.selectedIndex].value+"'"); if(restore) selObj.selectedIndex=0; } //                                                                                                                      International           -    Africa          -      Americas            -      Asia Pacific            -      Europe            -      Middle East               National              Nation Challenged              Politics              Business              Technology              Science              Health              Sports              New York Region              Education              Weather              Obituaries              NYT Front Page              Corrections                   Editorials/Op-Ed              Readers' Opinions

2002-04-20.title: Qaeda Leader in U.S. Custody Provokes Alert
2002-04-20.abstract: A leader of Al Qaeda now in American custody has told interrogators that terrorists had discussed plans for striking financial institutions in the United States.

2002-04-20.title: Venezuela's 2 Fateful Days: Leader Is Out, and In Again
2002-04-20.abstract: Article reviews two days in which Venezuelan Pres Hugo Chavez was ousted by business and military leaders, and then restored when pro-Chavez crowds demand return and uprising collapses; photos; chronology (M)

2002-04-20.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Getting Tougher On Donations
2002-04-20.abstract: New German law sets three-year jail terms for political party officials who try to hide illegal donations (S)

2002-04-21.title: Police in Belarus Detain 40 As a Protest Ends Violently
2002-04-21.abstract: Truncheon-wielding police officers in Belarus violently break up downtown march protesting living conditions and inadequate salaries; at least 40 demonstrators remain in jail; demonstrators' target is government of Pres Aleksandr Lukashenko, whose Communist-style government controls most aspects of country's laggard economy and permits only limited political opposition; Lukashenko has long been ostracized by most Western governments because of his government's human rights record; two leading political opponents have vanished in recent years(M)

2002-04-21.title: Germany Hears From Bitter Eastern Voters
2002-04-21.abstract: Anger and disillusionment in eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt is strong, 12 years after unification of Germany; about 20 percent of adults are jobless, and at least as many others have already moved to west; Communist-era factories have been shut down or reduced to tenth of their original size; disillusionment is expected to produce serious political consequences in upcoming state elections; biggest loser is expected to be Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's Social Democratic Party; biggest winners are likely to be successor to East Germany's Communist Party and center-right Christian Democratic Union; photos (M)

2002-04-21.title: British Warn of Plot to Assassinate Former Afghan King
2002-04-21.abstract: British military officials in Kabul warn that Mohammad Zahir Shah, former Afghan king who returned from exile, is under threat of assassination from killers posing as journalists; spokesman for Afghan government says government is aware of several such conspiracies, which he says is work of remaining Taliban and Al Qaeda fighters as well as other 'radical groups'; Afghan officials say they are confident they could protect former king; photo (M)

2002-04-21.title: Ex-Korean Chief's Daughter Rides Nostalgia Wave
2002-04-21.abstract: Park Geun Hye, South Korean congresswoman and daughter of late Pres Park Chung Hee, will announce formation of her own political party, with goal of winning presidency herself; her candidacy could split conservative votes, virtually guaranteeing election of Roh Moo Hyun, former human rights lawyer, whom Park dismisses as 'radical'; with voting still eight months away, many analysts are keeping their options open; photos (M)

2002-04-21.title: U.S. Official at Jenin Sees 'Terrible Human Tragedy'
2002-04-21.abstract: State Dept official William J Burns visits devastated Jenin refugee camp in West Bank and describes what he sees as 'terrible human tragedy'; camp's extensive destruction by Israeli forces has become focus of severe international criticism; Burns speaks of suffering of thousands of innocent Palestinian civilians; he does not criticize Israel, but he also makes no reference to Israel's argument that destruction in Jenin is result of fierce battle in which 23 Israeli soldiers were killed (M)

2002-04-22.title: Europeans Speak Out Against Rise of Le Pen
2002-04-22.abstract: Europeans of widely varying political persuasions today deplored the rise of Jean Le Pen and the National Front in France.

2002-04-22.title: Article 2002042291604945672 -- No Title
2002-04-22.abstract: function pop_me_up(pURL, features){new_window = window.open(pURL, "popup_window", features);new_window.focus();}  function changeImage(image_name,image_src) {document.images[image_name].src = image_src;}  function MM_jumpMenu(targ,selObj,restore){ //v3.0eval(targ+".location='"+selObj.options[selObj.selectedIndex].value+"'"); if(restore) selObj.selectedIndex=0; } //                                                                                                                      International           -      Africa            -      Americas            -      Asia Pacific            -    Europe          -      Middle East               National              Nation Challenged              Politics              Business              Technology              Science              Health              Sports              New York Region              Education              Weather              Obituaries              NYT Front Page              Corrections                   Editorials/Op-Ed              Readers' Opinions

2002-04-22.title: Conservatives Surge in German State Elections
2002-04-22.abstract: Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's governing Social Democrats fall to third place in state elections in eastern German state of Saxony-Anhalt, delivering jolt to Schroder's political fortunes; this is last major test of voter sentiment before national elections set for Sept 22; it appears to be resounding win for opposition Christian Democrats, with former Communists coming in second; some political commentators note that election focused on local issues, making it difficult to project result as sign of national discontent with ruling Social Democrats (M)

2002-04-22.title: MIDEAST TURMOIL: The Leaders; Personalities, Politics and Style
2002-04-22.abstract: Article compares and contrasts leadership styles of Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel and Palestian leader Yasir Arafat (M)

2002-04-22.title: MIDEAST TURMOIL: The Battle; A Different Fight, A Different Victory
2002-04-22.abstract: Israeli forces withdraw to edges of Nablus, and Palestinian fighters there return to streets, slapping posters on walls to celebrate their dead and vowing even fiercer struggle against Israel; this suggests that Israel may find it impossible to sustain its inroads, nor did it negate Palestinians' ability to fight back another day (M)

2002-04-23.title: Domino Effect? New Gain For Far Right in Europe
2002-04-23.abstract: Analysis: extreme right's strong showing in first round of French presidential election confirms rising law-and-order and anti-immigration sentiments in region and exposes electorate's deep disenchantment with France's tightly knit political elite; recent elections elsewhere in Western Europe have already signaled general turn to right, mirroring traditional pendulum swings of European politics; what analysts are alarmed about is recent shift of extreme rightist parties from periphery to center of political debate; photo (M)

2002-04-23.title: Article 2002042392192408391 -- No Title
2002-04-23.abstract: function pop_me_up(pURL, features){new_window = window.open(pURL, "popup_window", features);new_window.focus();}  function changeImage(image_name,image_src) {document.images[image_name].src = image_src;}  function MM_jumpMenu(targ,selObj,restore){ //v3.0eval(targ+".location='"+selObj.options[selObj.selectedIndex].value+"'"); if(restore) selObj.selectedIndex=0; } //                                                                                                                      International           -    Africa          -      Americas            -      Asia Pacific            -      Europe            -      Middle East               National              Nation Challenged              Politics              Business              Technology              Science              Health              Sports              New York Region              Education              Weather              Obituaries              NYT Front Page              Corrections                   Editorials/Op-Ed              Readers' Opinions

2002-04-23.title: Article 2002042392815288836 -- No Title
2002-04-23.abstract: function pop_me_up(pURL, features){new_window = window.open(pURL, "popup_window", features);new_window.focus();}  function changeImage(image_name,image_src) {document.images[image_name].src = image_src;}  function MM_jumpMenu(targ,selObj,restore){ //v3.0eval(targ+".location='"+selObj.options[selObj.selectedIndex].value+"'"); if(restore) selObj.selectedIndex=0; } //                                                                                                                      International           -      Africa            -    Americas          -      Asia Pacific            -      Europe            -      Middle East               National              Nation Challenged              Politics              Business              Technology              Science              Health              Sports              New York Region              Education              Weather              Obituaries              NYT Front Page              Corrections                   Editorials/Op-Ed              Readers' Opinions

2002-04-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: More Hindu-Muslim Violence
2002-04-23.abstract: Parts of Ahmedabad in India's western state of Gujarat are under curfew after renewed Hindu-Muslim violence (S)

2002-04-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: Pakistan: Challenge To The President
2002-04-23.abstract: Supreme Court of Pakistan begins hearing petitions by major political parties and Islamic religious groups challenging constitutionality of referendum called for Apr 30 by Pres Pervez Musharraf in move to extend his unelected presidency for five more years (S)

2002-04-24.title: Article 2002042490555449892 -- No Title
2002-04-24.abstract: The New York Times: International Text Version         function pop_me_up(pURL, features){new_window = window.open(pURL, "popup_window", features);new_window.focus();}  function changeImage(image_name,image_src) {document.images[image_name].src = image_src;}  function MM_jumpMenu(targ,selObj,restore){ //v3.0eval(targ+".location='"+selObj.options[selObj.selectedIndex].value+"'"); if(restore) selObj.selectedIndex=0; } //                                                                                                                         International           -      Africa            -      Americas            -      Asia Pacific            -      Europe            -      Middle East               National              Nation Challenged              Politics              Business              Technology              Science              Health              Sports              New York Region              Education              Weather              Obituaries              NYT Front Page              Corrections                   Editorials/Op-Ed              Readers' Opinions

2002-04-24.title: Article 2002042492983257422 -- No Title
2002-04-24.abstract: The New York Times: International Paper Version         function pop_me_up(pURL, features){new_window = window.open(pURL, "popup_window", features);new_window.focus();}  function changeImage(image_name,image_src) {document.images[image_name].src = image_src;}  function MM_jumpMenu(targ,selObj,restore){ //v3.0eval(targ+".location='"+selObj.options[selObj.selectedIndex].value+"'"); if(restore) selObj.selectedIndex=0; } //                                                                                                                         International           -      Africa            -      Americas            -      Asia Pacific            -      Europe            -      Middle East               National              Nation Challenged              Politics              Business              Technology              Science              Health              Sports              New York Region              Education              Weather              Obituaries              NYT Front Page              Corrections                   Editorials/Op-Ed              Readers' Opinions

2002-04-24.title: Argentina's President Suffers Double Blow
2002-04-24.abstract: Article on apparent unraveling of Argentina governmentnoted

2002-04-24.title: Explosion at Synagogue Tied to Jihad; Arrest Made
2002-04-24.abstract: Tunisian officials, initially reluctant to tie the blast to Islamic militants, now say that the act was planned and orchestrated by a man with suspected links to several cells.

2002-04-24.title: Kinshasa Journal; Suspects by the Score and, Oh, Such Digressions!
2002-04-24.abstract: Kinshasha Journal on mass military trial of 135 suspects that has so far shed no light on who was behind murder of Laurent D Kabila, former president who was gunned down in his palace last year; photos; map (M)

2002-04-25.title: Investigators Named In Canadian Deaths
2002-04-25.abstract: Air Force Brig Gen Stephen T Sargeant and Canadian Brig Gen Marc Dumais will lead inquiry into why Air National Guard F-16 pilot who thought he was under attack mistakenly bombed Canadian troops in Afghanistan last week, killing four of them and wounding eight others (S)

2002-04-25.title: Saudi to Warn Bush of Rupture Over Israel Policy
2002-04-25.abstract: Crown Prince Abdullah is expected to tell Pres Bush that strategic relationship between Saudi Arabia and US will be threatened if Bush does not moderate his support for Israel's military policies; two will meet on Apr 25; Saudi family has reportedly discussed using 'oil weapon' against US, or demanding that US leave strategic military bases in region; there is also warning of general drift by Arab leaders toward radical politics that have been building in Arab street; Saudi message contains undeniable brinkmanship intended to put pressure on Bush to take much larger political gamble by imposing peace settlement on Israelis and Palestinians; Abdullah is thought to represent not just Saudi Arabia but also broader voice of Arab world; his delegation's strong sense of alarm and crisis are being heard in other Arab capitals; Abdullah believes Bush has lost credibility by failing to follow through on his demand two weeks ago that Prime Min Ariel Sharon withdraw Israeli troops from West Bank and end sieges of Yasir Arafat's compound in Ramallah and of Church of Nativity in Bethlehem (M)

2002-04-25.title: Investigators Named in Canadian Deaths
2002-04-25.abstract: Brig. Gen. Stephen T. Sargeant of the Air Force and Brig. Gen. Marc Dumais of Canada will lead an inquiry into why an Air National Guard pilot mistakenly bombed Canadian troops in Afghanistan last week.

2002-04-25.title: Press Conference Following President's Meeting With Saudi Crown Prince
2002-04-25.abstract: Remarks made by President Bush following his meeting with Crown Prince Abdullah of Saudi Arabia on Thursday, as provided by the White House.

2002-04-25.title: Israel Eases Opposition to Inquiry Into Jenin Attack
2002-04-25.abstract: Israel on Wednesday seemed to retreat from the combative stance it had assumed against a United Nations team set to examine what happened at Jenin Refugee Camp.

2002-04-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: North Korea: Harsh Warning To U.S.
2002-04-26.abstract: North Korea's defense minister, Vice Marshal Kim Il Choi, harshly denounces United States in televised speech, with North Korean leader Kim Jong Il standing by; says North Korean soldiers would respond 'mercilessly' if US encroaches on North Korean land even 'one-thousandth of a millimeter' (S)

2002-04-26.title: World Briefing | Africa: Nigeria: Obasanjo To Seek Second Term
2002-04-26.abstract: Pres Olusegun Obasanjo of Nigeria says he will seek second term next year; photo (S)

2002-04-26.title: Hunger in Southern Africa Imperils Lives of Millions
2002-04-26.abstract: United Nations World Food Program reports that southern Africa's worst food shortage in decade is spreading and more than five million people across region may now need help; agency is already feeding 2.6 million people, and millions more are thought to be falling deeper into hunger; Malawi, hardest hit, has declared state of disaster; Zambia and Zimbabwe are in dire trouble also, and Mozambique and Swaziland will be hard pressed to avoid similar shortages; UN agency says 145,000 tons of food, worth $69 million, is needed in coming months, but that only $3 million worth of food has been pledged so far; erratic weather for last few years laid foundations for current crisis (M)

2002-04-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Top Chechen Reported Killed
2002-04-26.abstract: Russia's Federal Security Service says it killed Arab-born Chechen field commander known as Khattab, suspected of links to Al Qaeda, in operation in mid-March; past reports of Khattab's death have proved false; he is accused of torturing and executing Russian prisoners (S)

2002-04-26.title: SAUDI TELLS BUSH U.S. MUST TEMPER BACKING OF ISRAEL
2002-04-26.abstract: Crown Prince Abdullah of Saudi Arabia tells Pres Bush bluntly that United States must temper its support for Israel or face grave consequences throughout Arab world; Saudi officials at meeting at president's ranch in Texas say Abdullah warned that US would continue to lose credibility in Middle East and create more instability there unless it did more to stop Israel's incursions into Palestinian areas; Adel al-Jubeir, Abdullah's foreign policy adviser, says Prime Min Ariel Sharon wants to drag region 'over a cliff'; American officials offer far more positive account of meetings; Pres Bush says he and crown prince formed 'strong personal bond'; both Bush and Jubeir say crown prince did not threaten in any way to reduce Saudi oil exports to US; Bush, who infuriated Arab world week ago by calling Sharon 'a man of peace,' says he told Abdullah he is counting on Israel to withdraw forces from Palestinian areas, including resolving standoffs in Ramallah and Bethlehem; photo (M)

2002-04-27.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: An Apology From The President
2002-04-27.abstract: South Korea Pres Kim Dae Jung apologizes for scandals surrounding his sons and former aides; apology comes on eve of convention to nominate presidential candidate; photo (S)

2002-04-27.title: Overseas Americans : 4 associations to lobby for rights in Washington
2002-04-27.abstract: WASHINGTON—  CORRECTION PUBLISHED IN THE INTERNATIONAL HERALD TRIBUNE DATED Saturday, May 25, 2002:

2002-04-27.title: Security precautions : Should you let your government know where you are?
2002-04-27.abstract: PARIS—  An earthquake strikes. A government collapses. A terrorist threat emerges. Relatives try to inform you that a family member is dying. Or you just lose your passport.

2002-04-27.title: Given $300,000, Abductors Refuse to Free 2 Americans
2002-04-27.abstract: Philippine and American officials say Abu Sayyaf, Muslim extremist group that is holding two Americans hostage in southern Philippines, refuses to hand them over after being paid $300,000 ransom, and has demanded another $200,000; original ransom money was raised and paid by families of hostages, Martin and Gracia Burnham, with acquiescence of Bush administration; Philippine soldiers and American Special Forces are conducting exercises on island of Basilan, where Burnhams are last known to be held, and Philippine officials now say that military activity may have impeded Burnhams' release (M)

2002-04-27.title: Language barriers in Lebanon : Letters to the editor
2002-04-27.abstract: Regarding "Coping in a strange, new world" (Feb. 16-17):

2002-04-28.title: Pakistani Court Backs President On Effort to Extend His Term
2002-04-28.abstract: Pakistaan's Supreme Courtrules upcoming April 30 referendum to extend presidency of Pervez Musharraf is constitutional and can proceed as planned; unanimously rejects three petitions from opposition parties and lawyers' association; photo (M)

2002-04-28.title: At Least 100,000 in France Protest Far Rightist
2002-04-28.abstract: Estimated 100,000 demonstrators steam through streets of Paris and other French cities in further protest against strong showing of far-right leader Jean-Marie Le Pen in first round of presidential balloting; demonstrations draw impromptu alliance of human rights groups and antiracism, student and labor organizations opposed to Le Pen and his anti-immigrant policies and proposals for mass deportations of foreigners without legal residence in France (S)

2002-04-28.title: Musharraf Treading Gently Against Pakistani Militants
2002-04-28.abstract: Doubts arise in Pakistan over commitment of Pres Pervez Musharraf to follow through on pledge made three months ago to crack down on violent Muslim groups and on religious schools that breed them; of nearly 2,000 people arrested in sweep of Islamic radicals, as many as 70 percent have been set free; after five militant groups were banned, members reorganized under new names; reform of religious academies has met with strong resistance, and schools continue to operate much as they always have; while Musharraf has not backed away from major policy reversal of choosing to support US in war against terrorism, direction of his policies are not so clear on most sensitive and entrenched domestic issues; photo (M)

2002-04-28.title: Deep Shock In Germany, Where Guns Are Rare
2002-04-28.abstract: Germans struggle to comprehend murderous shooting rampage by 19-year-old Robert Steinhauser, which left 18 students and teachers and himself dead and 10 others wounded at high school in Erfurt, since country has some of toughest gun restrictions in world; police report Steinhauser acquired weapons entirely legally as member of officially accredited sport shooting club; incident underscores that such violence is more feasible than Germans have assumed; Steinhauser was expelled from school earlier in year for forging doctors letters with which he had hoped to delay meeting graduation requirements; photo (M)

2002-04-28.title: Plane-Spotters Found Guilty In Greek Court In Secrets Case
2002-04-28.abstract: Group of British tourists pursuing hobby of plane-spotting, peculiarly favorite pasttime in Britain, are convicted in Greek court in Kalamata of obtaining national secrets; eight defendants are sentenced to three years in jail and six others to one-year suspended sentences; reactions in Britain range from incredulity to outrage; member of Parliament decries case as farce; defendants were arrested in November at military air base air show to which they were invited; were originally charged with more serious crime of espionage, which carries 20-year sentence; Greece bans photography of military installations (M)

2002-04-29.title: Moscow Journal; Feast or Fast? Russians Just Can't Give Up Lent
2002-04-29.abstract: Many Russians are recapturing Lenten traditions, after 70 years in which Bolsheviks destroyed thousands of churches killing or persecuting millions of believers and after decade of wild-east capitalism and noveau-riche flamboyance; even though government officials and private citizens are reverting to fasting and to Lenten diets of meatless and dairy-free meals, ascetic impulse often collides with epicurean and regimen is not always strict; photos (M)

2002-04-29.title: The Hurdles And the Goal
2002-04-29.abstract: News analysis; Pres Bush's winning of Israeli agreement to end siege of Yasir Arafat's compound in Ramallah may be important step to shore up American credibility with Arab world by repositioning American foreign policy in Middle East, but volatile crisis is far from over and he faces even more difficult challenge of getting warring parties back to negotiating table where expectations for American pressure remain high on both sides; photo (M)

2002-04-29.title: More Reunions Unite Koreans, But Politics Still Intrude
2002-04-29.abstract: North Korea welcomes group of visitors from South Korea, whose families were divided by Korean War, but also calls for resignation of South Korea's Foreign Minister Choi Sung Hong for backing Pres Bush's tough stance toward North Korea, which Bush contends is part of 'axis of evil' (M)

2002-04-29.title: In Bush's Words: Arafat Must 'Thwart' Terrorism
2002-04-29.abstract: Excerpts from remarks by Pres Bush regarding Israel's acceptance of compromise to end siege of compound of Yasir Arafat in Ramallah on West Bank (S)

2002-04-29.title: Ignoring Risk, Afghan Refugees Rush Home
2002-04-29.abstract: Thousands of Afghan refugees are returning home from Pakistan, where they fled at onset of Soviet invasion in 1979; more than 300,000 have migrated home in two months since UN refugee agency began assisting repatriation; refugee officials believe as many as 1.2 million could also return from Pakistan and Iran; say it could take three years, perhaps longer, before estimated 5 to 6 million in neighboring countries return; those returning home seem to be ingoring naysayers who worry about risk of anarchy if new government in Kabul fails to rein in warlords who currently control much of country, or if Taliban and Al Qaeda regroup for new guerrilla war; map; photos (L)

2002-04-30.title: Back in Business, Argentina Calms Down and the Peso Perks Up
2002-04-30.abstract: Argentina passes first day without turmoil on April 29 following swearing-in of new Economy Minister Roberto Lavagna on April 27; banks and stock market are open; pesos are again exchanged legally for dollars; Lavagna persuades Pres Eduardo Duhalde to allow currency to continue floating against dollar and to abandon, at least for moment, plan for fixed exchange rate that analysts warn cannot be maintained (M)

2002-04-30.title: Havana Journal; Masonic Rites, No Less. In Castro's Bailiwick!
2002-04-30.abstract: Article on Masonic Supreme Council, largest of Cuba's fraternal organizations whose membership has more than doubled since 1980's to more than 29,000 members after long period of distrust following Communist revolution in 1959, when Masons were seen as threat and treated as clandestine counter-revolutionary sect; government's current official policy links Masons to some of nobler moments of Cuba's past, even though Masons complain privately of infiltration by agents and of veiled warnings about meetings with foreigners, including American diplomats; Jesus Armada Pena, council's sovereign grand commander, comments; photo (M)

2002-04-30.title: Pakistanis Vote on Extending Musharraf's Term
2002-04-30.abstract: President Pervez Musharraf, who seized power in a nonviolent coup in 1999, sought to continue his leadership today in a popular referendum.

2002-04-30.title: Youth Let Their Thumbs Do the Talking in Japan
2002-04-30.abstract: Young Japanese in quiet technology-driven change are developing hyperagile thumbs, fruit of childhoods spent furiously thumbing hand-held computer games and young adulthoods thumbing out e-mail messages on cellphone key pads; study of cellphone habits of people in eight major world cities finds Japan's 'thumb generation' is most advanced in world; photos (M)

2002-04-30.title: Gibraltar Residents Fight for Identity
2002-04-30.abstract: Increasingly likely prospects that Britain and Spain may agree to joint sovereignty over Gibraltar, solidly British since Spanish crown lost it in 1704, fans outrage and deep anxiety among Rock's 30,000 residents, who harbor deep mistrust of Spain; map; photo (M)
